default_scene.note: InteractionsFoldingAir samples beta_logup from TranscriptBus at tidx_if (handed in from GkrInputAir) and broadcasts it on BatchConstraintConductorBus for downstream consumers.

input-handoff.note: GkrInputAir hands off tidx_if on InteractionsFoldingInputBus; InteractionsFoldingAir consumes it to know where to place its transcript sample.

transcript-sample.note: beta_logup is a TranscriptBus sample (is_sample=1). Interleaved within GKR's range but at a distinct tidx, so no collision with alpha_logup.

broadcast.note: BatchConstraintConductorBus is the BC module's challenge-distribution bus. beta_logup is placed here for InteractionsFoldingAir's own folding rows in bc-13.

bc-1.constraints_html: InteractionsFoldingAir receives tidx_if from GkrInputAir via InteractionsFoldingInputBus, samples beta_logup on TranscriptBus at that position, and places it on BatchConstraintConductorBus for later consumption.
bc-1.soundness_html: Although BC's beta sample is interleaved into GKR's transcript range, TranscriptBus's per-tidx ownership ensures no collision: GKR's alpha is at tidx_if − D_EF, BC's beta is at tidx_if. The sponge state at tidx_if absorbs alpha (GKR's sample) first, so beta is a deterministic function of everything before it.

default_scene.note: ConstraintsFoldingAir samples lambda on TranscriptBus at tidx_cf (handed in from GkrInputAir via ConstraintsFoldingInputBus) and publishes it on BatchConstraintConductorBus for downstream constraint folding.

input-handoff.note: GkrInputAir passes tidx_cf (start of BC's contiguous region) to ConstraintsFoldingAir via ConstraintsFoldingInputBus.

transcript-sample.note: lambda is sampled at tidx_cf and pins all downstream constraint-folding randomness.

broadcast.note: lambda travels on BatchConstraintConductorBus to every consumer (per-row lambda values on ConstraintsFoldingAir are looked up from this bus).

bc-2.constraints_html: ConstraintsFoldingAir receives tidx_cf from GkrInputAir, samples lambda on TranscriptBus at tidx_cf, and distributes it via BatchConstraintConductorBus.
bc-2.soundness_html: lambda is a transcript sample pinned by the sponge, and its tidx is a deterministic function of GKR's outputs. BatchConstraintConductorBus is the distribution mechanism for challenges throughout the BC module; each consumer receives lambda at a known tidx.

default_scene.note: FractionsFolderAir iterates per present AIR with air_idx decrementing to 0, observing (sum_claim_p[i], sum_claim_q[i]) on TranscriptBus each row. The aggregate cur_p_sum/cur_q_sum are checked against gkr_input_layer_claim received on BatchConstraintModuleBus.

row-count.note: FractionFolderInputBus hands in num_present_airs; air_idx decrements per row and must reach 0 on the last row, forcing exactly num_present_airs rows.

observe.note: Each per-AIR row absorbs (sum_claim_p[i], sum_claim_q[i]) into the transcript as two consecutive value observations.

gkr-consistency.note: On the last row the running sums (cur_p_sum, cur_q_sum) must equal [p_xi_claim, q_xi_claim − alpha_logup] handed in on BatchConstraintModuleBus from GkrInputAir.

transition.note: Adjacent rows: air_idx decreases by 1 and running sums accumulate the just-observed (sum_claim_p, sum_claim_q) terms.

bc-3.constraints_html: FractionsFolderAir receives num_present_airs from ProofShapeAir via FractionFolderInputBus and gkr_claim via BatchConstraintModuleBus. It decrements air_idx by 1 per row, reaching 0 on the last row (forcing exactly num_present_airs rows). For each row, it observes (numerator_term[i], denominator_term[i]) on TranscriptBus and, in aggregate, checks p_xi_claim = Σ numerator_term[i] == 0 and q_xi_claim = Σ denominator_term[i] == alpha_logup.
bc-3.soundness_html: The row count is forced by the FractionFolderInputBus handoff (matching ProofShapeAir's num_present_airs). The aggregate equality p_xi_claim == 0, q_xi_claim == alpha_logup is the defining LogUp/fractional-sumcheck identity at xi. Per-AIR observations are pinned on the transcript.

default_scene.note: After the per-AIR observations, FractionsFolderAir samples mu on TranscriptBus and Horner-accumulates sum_claim = Σ_k μ^k * x_k across interleaved numerator/denominator terms (cur_hash). mu is placed on BatchConstraintConductorBus.

mu-sample.note: mu is sampled on TranscriptBus at the tidx immediately after the last (sum_claim_p, sum_claim_q) observation in bc-3.

horner-claim.note: cur_hash is the Horner accumulator for Σ_k μ^k x_k; the last row carries sum_claim, handed to the sumcheck pipeline on SumcheckClaimBus (round 0).

broadcast.note: BatchConstraintConductorBus distributes mu to ExpressionClaimAir (bc-14), which folds all per-AIR claims by identical mu powers.

bc-4.constraints_html: FractionsFolderAir samples mu on TranscriptBus after the per-AIR observations. It computes sum_claim = Σ_k μ^k * x_k interleaving numerator and denominator terms, and broadcasts mu on BatchConstraintConductorBus.
bc-4.soundness_html: mu is a transcript sample at a fixed tidx. The sum is computed via algebraic constraints within the AIR; the resulting sum_claim is the input to the sumcheck pipeline.

default_scene.note: UnivariateSumcheckAir iterates coeff_idx from univariate_deg down to 0, observing each coefficient on TranscriptBus, accumulating sum_at_roots over the roots-of-unity domain, then sampling r and Horner-evaluating s_0(r) in value_at_r.

coeff-count.note: coeff_idx starts at univariate_deg = (max_constraint_degree + 1) * (2^l_skip − 1) and decrements by 1 to 0, forcing exactly univariate_deg + 1 coefficient observations. The count is VK-fixed.

transcript-observe.note: Each row observes one coefficient of the univariate polynomial s_0 on TranscriptBus.

domain-check.note: eq_to_1 selects the roots-of-unity contributions so sum_at_roots accumulates |D| * (a_0 + a_{|D|}). On the last row sum_at_roots must match the sum_claim received on SumcheckClaimBus.

horner-at-r.note: After sampling r, value_at_r Horner-evaluates s_0(r_0). The last-row value is forwarded as the next sumcheck claim.

r-output.note: r_0 is simultaneously sampled on TranscriptBus and published on ConstraintSumcheckRandomnessBus (idx=0) so Stacking and the eq polynomial AIRs can consume it.

bc-5.constraints_html: UnivariateSumcheckAir has coeff_idx starting at univariate_deg = (max_constraint_degree + 1) * (2^l_skip − 1), decrementing by 1 each row, reaching 0 on the last row (forcing univariate_deg + 1 coeffs). On the first row it receives sum_claim on SumcheckClaimBus; it observes each coefficient on TranscriptBus, aggregates the domain sum check, samples r, evaluates via Horner, and sends the claim forward on SumcheckClaimBus. r_0 is also sent on ConstraintSumcheckRandomnessBus.
bc-5.soundness_html: The coeff count is VK-determined (forces exactly univariate_deg + 1 coeffs, verify_proof_shape line 433). The |D| * (a_0 + a_{|D|}) identity is the univariate sumcheck check at the roots-of-unity domain. Horner evaluation is algebraically enforced. r_0 is transcript-pinned.

default_scene.note: MultilinearSumcheckAir iterates n_max rounds. Within each round, eval_idx goes 0..s_deg evaluating the round polynomial; the s(0)+s(1) check equates cur_sum to the incoming claim, and the Lagrange interpolation at r produces the next round's claim.

per-round-shape.note: Each round has s_deg + 1 rows; eval_idx increments from 0 and must reach s_deg on the last eval row (verify_proof_shape line 457).

observe.note: Each evaluation point is absorbed into TranscriptBus before interpolation.

sum-check.note: s(0) + s(1) == claim pins the round polynomial to the running sumcheck claim received on SumcheckClaimBus.

interpolate.note: prefix/suffix/lagrange/denom_inv Lagrange-interpolate s_j at r_j; the last eval row publishes the next round's claim.

bc-6.constraints_html: Per round, eval_idx starts at 0, increments by 1, and must reach s_deg = max_constraint_degree + 1 on the last eval (verify_proof_shape line 457). Each round: receive claim on SumcheckClaimBus, observe evals on TranscriptBus, verify s(0) + s(1) == claim, sample r_j, interpolate at r_j via Lagrange, send next claim. n_max is forced by the ExpressionClaimNMaxBus → ExpressionClaimAir → SumcheckClaimBus chain.
bc-6.soundness_html: Round count is exactly n_max (forced by chain ending at ExpressionClaimAir's final claim send, verify_proof_shape line 440). Per-round degree is VK-fixed. The s(0)+s(1) identity is the multilinear sumcheck round check. Challenges r_j are transcript-pinned and sent on ConstraintSumcheckRandomnessBus to Stacking.

default_scene.note: Stacking's OpeningClaimsAir observes each (col_claim, rot_claim) on TranscriptBus and republishes them on ColumnClaimsBus so SymbolicExpressionAir can resolve DAG variables to the same opening values.

rows._note: was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

connections.label: resolve variable
connections._note: was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

transcript-observe.note: Each column opening value is absorbed into TranscriptBus as a non-sample observation. rot_claim is observed only when need_rot=1 for that partition.

column-claims-publish.note: ColumnClaimsBus is a permutation bus keyed by (sort_idx, part_idx, col_idx, is_rot). OpeningClaimsAir sends; SymbolicExpressionAir and the folding AIRs receive.

rows._note: was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

connections.label: variable node
connections._note: was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

same-cell.note: Critical invariant: the same col_claim cell is both TranscriptBus-observed and ColumnClaimsBus-sent, forcing the transcript value to equal the opening resolved during DAG evaluation.

bc-7.constraints_html: Note: in the circuit, column opening observations are performed by Stacking's OpeningClaimsAir (phase A of Stacking schedule), though the reference verifier performs them in verify_zerocheck_and_logup. OpeningClaimsAir observes each (col_claim, rot_claim) on TranscriptBus (rot_claim only when need_rot = 1) and sends them on ColumnClaimsBus. SymbolicExpressionAir consumes these when resolving variables in the DAG.
bc-7.soundness_html: Observations are pinned on TranscriptBus. ColumnClaimsBus is a permutation bus, so the per-AIR per-partition values used in constraint evaluation equal the transcript-observed values. The need_rot flag from AirShapeBus gates rot_claim, and when need_rot = 0, rot_claim is constrained to zero.

default_scene.note: Eq3bAir iterates bit positions of each interaction's stacked b index, looking up xi coordinates on XiRandomnessBus and accumulating eq = ∏ eq(xi_n, b_n) in the eq column. The final per-interaction eq is published on Eq3bBus.

shape-handoff.note: Eq3bShapeBus hands in (sort_idx, n_lift, n_logup, num_interactions) from ProofShapeAir so Eq3bAir knows how many bits and interactions to cover.

rows._note: was 'num_interactions', best-match to interaction_idx + has_no_interactions (derived in Eq3bAir eval as interaction_idx + not(has_no_interactions))

connections._note: was 'num_interactions', best-match to interaction_idx + has_no_interactions (derived in Eq3bAir eval as interaction_idx + not(has_no_interactions))

xi-lookup.note: For each bit position n in [n_lift, n_logup), Eq3bAir looks up xi[l_skip + n] on XiRandomnessBus (written by GKR).

eq-accumulate.note: The eq column runs the product eq(xi_n, b_n) bit by bit. idx reconstructs the stacked index from nth_bit for bus-balance with Eq3bShapeBus.

publish.note: The final accumulated eq per interaction is published on Eq3bBus; InteractionsFoldingAir consumes it to weight interaction denominators (bc-13).

bc-8.constraints_html: Eq3bAir receives (i, n_lift, n_logup, num_interactions) on Eq3bShapeBus and the relevant xi coordinates on XiRandomnessBus. It computes eq_3b values for each interaction's stacked b index and sends them on Eq3bBus to InteractionsFoldingAir.
bc-8.soundness_html: The b indices are derived from VK structure and interaction idx; xi coords are transcript-pinned (via GKR, T). Eq3bAir's constraints enforce the multilinear eq polynomial evaluation algebraically. Bus balancing forces the correct count.

default_scene.note: EqNsAir multiplies a base univariate factor (eval_eq_uni from EqUniAir, or eval_eq_sharp_uni from EqSharpUniAir→EqSharpUniReceiverAir) with multilinear eq factors eq(xi[l_skip+n-1], r_n) per dimension, front-loading by an r_product suffix to broadcast eq_ns/eq_sharp_ns on EqNOuterBus.

base-eq-uni.note: EqUniAir squares xi[0] and r_0 repeatedly (x, y = xi_0^{2^idx}, r_0^{2^idx}) accumulating res = eval_eq_uni(l_skip, xi[0], r_0). The last row publishes the final value on EqZeroNBus to EqNsAir.

eq-sharp.note: EqSharpUniAir does the butterfly decomposition of eval_eq_sharp_uni over roots of unity; EqSharpUniReceiverAir Horner-sums the coefficient stream at r_0 to obtain the final sharp value, published on EqZeroNBus with is_sharp=1.

multiplicative-recursion.note: Each EqNsAir row multiplies eq and eq_sharp by eq(xi[l_skip+n-1], r_n) and absorbs a trailing r factor (r_product). Every intermediate eq_ns[n] is broadcast on EqNOuterBus so constraint folding and ExpressionClaimAir can pick the right dimension.

randomness-lookup.note: Per row, xi_n and r_n are looked up from the shared challenge buses so the multiplicative step uses the same values as sampled during GKR (xi) and sumcheck (r).

bc-9.constraints_html: EqUniAir computes eval_eq_uni(l_skip, xi[0], r_0) (base factor). EqSharpUniAir + EqSharpUniReceiverAir compute eval_eq_sharp_uni with roots of unity (accumulator). EqNsAir multiplies these with eval_eq_mle([xi[l_skip+j-1]], [r_j]) for j = 1..i and a trailing r product (front-loading). Results are sent on EqNOuterBus to ConstraintsFoldingAir and ExpressionClaimAir; on SelHypercubeBus to SymbolicExpressionAir.
bc-9.soundness_html: All eq polynomial formulas are algebraically constrained. xi and r are transcript-pinned. PowerCheckerBus provides roots-of-unity powers (LT). Front-loading the batch correction prod(r_j for j > i) avoids redundant computation while preserving correctness.

default_scene.note: EqNegAir handles AIRs with n < 0. For each negative section, it iteratively squares u, r, and r*omega (given on EqNegBaseRandBus by Stacking's EqBaseAir), accumulating eq kernels, and outputs both the eq result and sel_uni(n, r_0) values.

base-rand.note: Stacking's EqBaseAir provides u (= u[n_lift]) and r_squared (= r_0^2) on EqNegBaseRandBus; EqNegAir consumes them as seeds for the squaring ladder.

squaring-ladder.note: Each row squares u_pow, r_pow, r_omega_pow — prod_u_r and prod_u_r_omega accumulate the eq and k_rot kernels across the negative dimension's |n| squaring steps.

eq-result.note: The final prod_u_r and prod_u_r_omega per negative section are emitted on EqNegResultBus — Stacking's EqBaseAir consumes them as its eq-neg factor.

sel-uni-publish.note: For each negative dimension, sel_uni(n, r_0) is sent on SelUniBus so SymbolicExpressionAir can look up the correct is_first/is_last selector when evaluating AIRs with log_height < l_skip.

bc-10.constraints_html: EqNegAir receives (u_0, r_0^2) on EqNegBaseRandBus from Stacking's EqBaseAir and computes eq_neg evaluations for each negative-n dimension. It sends results on EqNegResultBus (to EqBaseAir) and sel_uni(n, r_0) values on SelUniBus (to SymbolicExpressionAir).
bc-10.soundness_html: Negative-dimension handling is the lift adjustment described in M-BC(c): use l = l_skip + n and r_0^{2^|n|}. The shared computation with Stacking (via EqNegBaseRandBus/EqNegResultBus) eliminates duplication while a single AIR enforces the arithmetic.

default_scene.note: SymbolicExpressionAir walks the cached DAG one node per row. kind encodes node type (const, variable, public-value, arithmetic op, etc.); args are looked up from earlier rows via SymbolicExpressionBus; variables resolve via ColumnClaimsBus and PublicValuesBus; is_first/is_last selectors come from SelHypercubeBus / SelUniBus.

rows._note: was 'kind', best-match to flags (NodeKind encoded via Encoder in the flags array, not a separate column); was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

was 'kind', best-match to flags (NodeKind encoded via Encoder in the flags array, not a separate column); was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

was 'kind', best-match to flags (NodeKind encoded via Encoder in the flags array, not a separate column); was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

connections.label: publish node
connections._note: was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

connections.label: variable kind
connections._note: was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

dag-walk.note: One row per DAG node: each computes value from args (children), kind (op type), and attrs (e.g. column index). fanout counts parent references so the row is sent on SymbolicExpressionBus with multiplicity fanout.

rows._note: was 'kind', best-match to flags (NodeKind encoded via Encoder in the flags array, not a separate column); was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

was 'kind', best-match to flags (NodeKind encoded via Encoder in the flags array, not a separate column); was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

connections.label: fanout=1..k
connections._note: was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

variable-resolve.note: When kind = variable, attrs holds (sort_idx, part_idx, col_idx, is_rot) and the value is looked up on ColumnClaimsBus — the same permutation bus populated by OpeningClaimsAir (bc-7).

rows._note: was 'kind', best-match to flags (NodeKind encoded via Encoder in the flags array, not a separate column); was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

connections.label: claim
connections._note: was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

public-value.note: When kind = public_value, attrs encodes (air_idx, pv_idx) and the value is looked up on PublicValuesBus (published in preamble-6).

rows._note: was 'kind', best-match to flags (NodeKind encoded via Encoder in the flags array, not a separate column); was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

connections._note: was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

selectors.note: is_first_row / is_last_row / is_transition selectors are looked up on SelHypercubeBus (for n >= 0, provided by EqNsAir) or SelUniBus (for n < 0, provided by EqNegAir).

rows._note: was 'kind', best-match to flags (NodeKind encoded via Encoder in the flags array, not a separate column); was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

connections.label: n >= 0
connections._note: was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

connections.label: n < 0
connections._note: was 'value', best-match to args (node value is computed per-row from args/attrs/flags; for variable/public-value nodes the value is taken from args[..D_EF], no dedicated value column)

bc-11.constraints_html: SymbolicExpressionAir evaluates the DAG node-by-node (constants, arithmetic ops, variables from ColumnClaimsBus (offset 0 = value, offset 1 = rotated), public values from PublicValuesBus). Selectors is_first_row/is_last_row/is_transition are computed using SelHypercubeBus (n >= 0, from EqNsAir) and SelUniBus (n < 0, from EqNegAir). The cached trace encodes the DAG structure.
bc-11.soundness_html: The DAG structure is committed via the SymbolicExpressionAir cached trace commitment, observed in ProofShape's preamble (second assumption of the main claim: commitment encodes vk_child). Variables resolve to transcript-observed column openings (bc-7) and public values (preamble-6). Selectors are computed correctly by EqNsAir and EqNegAir. Therefore the DAG evaluation equals the reference verifier's VerifierConstraintEvaluator output.

default_scene.note: ConstraintsFoldingAir ingests per-constraint evaluations from SymbolicExpressionAir via ConstraintsFoldingBus, Horner-folds them with lambda powers into cur_sum, and multiplies the final per-AIR sum by eq_n = eq_ns[n_lift] (looked up via EqNOuterBus). The weighted claim is forwarded on ExpressionClaimBus.

receive-constraints.note: SymbolicExpressionAir evaluates constraint nodes and sends their (air_idx, constraint_idx, value) tuples on ConstraintsFoldingBus; ConstraintsFoldingAir consumes one per row.

lambda-fold.note: cur_sum accumulates Σ_j λ^j * value_j via a Horner step each row: new_sum = sum * lambda + value. lambda is looked up from BatchConstraintConductorBus.

eq-weight.note: On the last row of each AIR, cur_sum is multiplied by eq_n (= eq_ns[n_lift]), looked up from EqNOuterBus. The result is the per-AIR numerator_term.

publish.note: ExpressionClaimBus carries the weighted per-AIR claim to ExpressionClaimAir with is_interaction=0 (distinguishing it from the interaction-folding stream).

bc-12.constraints_html: ConstraintsFoldingAir receives per-constraint evaluations from SymbolicExpressionAir, folds with lambda powers (BatchConstraintConductorBus), weights by eq_ns (EqNOuterBus from EqNsAir) with n_lift (NLiftBus from ProofShapeAir), and sends the resulting claim on ExpressionClaimBus to ExpressionClaimAir.
bc-12.soundness_html: Lambda-folding is algebraically constrained. eq_ns correctly weights by the multilinear equality factor at r. Bus balancing ensures each AIR contributes exactly one numerator_term.

default_scene.note: InteractionsFoldingAir builds each interaction's (num, denom) from message components received on InteractionsFoldingBus, Horner-folds by beta, weights by eq_3b per interaction, and aggregates into (final_acc_num, final_acc_denom) to publish on ExpressionClaimBus with is_interaction=1.

message-receive.note: Each message component (count, msg_0, msg_1, ..., bus_idx) arrives on InteractionsFoldingBus; is_first_msg, is_second_msg, is_bus_idx row-markers route them into num vs denom buckets.

beta-fold.note: cur_sum = cur_sum * beta + value Horner-folds the message stream; the bus_idx row adds (bus_idx + 1) * beta^{msg_len} as the domain-separation offset.

eq3b-weight.note: For each interaction, eq_3b[sort_idx][interaction_idx] is looked up from Eq3bBus and applied to both num and denom before aggregating.

publish.note: Last row of each AIR publishes (final_acc_num, final_acc_denom) on ExpressionClaimBus with is_interaction=1, one pair per present AIR.

bc-13.constraints_html: InteractionsFoldingAir receives interaction evaluations from SymbolicExpressionAir, folds with beta_logup powers, weights each interaction by eq_3b (from Eq3bBus), aggregates per-AIR totals, weights by eq_sharp_ns, and sends denominator_term[i] on ExpressionClaimBus.
bc-13.soundness_html: Beta-folding is algebraically constrained; the bus_index offset enforces per-interaction domain separation. eq_3b selects the correct stacked b index within the logup dimension; eq_sharp_ns applies the sharp univariate eq factor.

default_scene.note: ExpressionClaimAir aggregates per-AIR numerator/denominator claims from ExpressionClaimBus, Horner-folds by mu (from BatchConstraintConductorBus), multiplies by normalization (eq_ns, hyperdim, pow) in multiplier, and hands the final cur_sum = evaluated_claim to the sumcheck pipeline on SumcheckClaimBus at round n_max.

rows._note: was 'is_interaction', best-match to group_idx (is_interaction = 1 - group_idx, not a stored column)
was 'is_interaction', best-match to group_idx (is_interaction = 1 - group_idx, not a stored column)

ingest-claims.note: Each row consumes one (is_interaction, idx, value) entry from ExpressionClaimBus produced by bc-12 / bc-13. The is_interaction bit routes into numerator vs denominator stream via idx_parity.

rows._note: was 'is_interaction', best-match to group_idx (is_interaction = 1 - group_idx, not a stored column)
was 'is_interaction', best-match to group_idx (is_interaction = 1 - group_idx, not a stored column)

mu-fold.note: cur_sum Horner-folds all inputs with mu (from BatchConstraintConductorBus) — identical to the mu power used in FractionsFolderAir (bc-4).

final-claim.note: Last row sends cur_sum = evaluated_claim on SumcheckClaimBus at round = n_max. The multilinear sumcheck chain (bc-6) must terminate at this same value, making the identity cur_sum == evaluated_claim bus-balanced.

bc-14.constraints_html: ExpressionClaimAir receives per-AIR (numerator_term, denominator_term) claims on ExpressionClaimBus, batches them with mu powers (from BatchConstraintConductorBus), applies norm_factor and eq_sharp_ns accounting, and sends the final evaluated_claim on SumcheckClaimBus at round n_max. MultilinearSumcheckAir's chain ends at this final claim, so cur_sum (sumcheck output) == evaluated_claim.
bc-14.soundness_html: This is the defining batch-constraint identity: the multilinear sumcheck chain terminates at ExpressionClaimAir's computed evaluated_claim. Because SumcheckClaimBus is a permutation bus threading through all sumcheck rounds, if sum does not match, the bus is unbalanced. ExpressionClaimNMaxBus forces n_max rounds.

default_scene.note: BC's outputs to Stacking: UnivariateSumcheckAir sends r_0 on ConstraintSumcheckRandomnessBus (idx=0), MultilinearSumcheckAir sends r_1..r_{n_max}. MultilinearSumcheckAir also sends tidx_stack on StackingModuleBus at the end of schedule (6).

randomness-vector.note: n_max + 1 r values are broadcast on ConstraintSumcheckRandomnessBus indexed 0..n_max. Stacking's sumcheck AIRs consume them at the same indices for eq polynomial evaluation.

tidx-handoff.note: StackingModuleBus carries tidx_stack, the transcript index just after BC's contiguous region (2)-(6). Stacking's OpeningClaimsAir uses it as its starting tidx.

bc-15.constraints_html: UnivariateSumcheckAir sends r_0 on ConstraintSumcheckRandomnessBus; MultilinearSumcheckAir sends r_1..r_{n_max}. MultilinearSumcheckAir (on its last round) sends (tidx_stack, ...) on StackingModuleBus. tidx_stack is computed from tidx_cf by summing schedule (2)-(6) slot counts.
bc-15.soundness_html: The randomness vector is exactly the transcript samples from bc-5/bc-6, pinned by the sponge (T). Bus balancing ensures exactly n_max + 1 r values. The tidx_stack handoff is deterministic.
